title: medicago minima var. incana
intro: Medicago minima var. incana, also known as hairy little lucerne or hairy annual medick, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and flowers, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Medicago minima var. incana include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions or decoctions, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, digestive problems, and other health issues.
